target: data/sections.json — surgical href patches across part1, part4, part5, part6, part7, part8 to replace 18 part-level fallback anchors (href="#partN") with subsection-precise anchors (href="#subsectionId") where the link text claims a specific X.Y subsection AND a matching subsection id exists. Closes the moderate "imprecise part-level hrefs" finding from HOL-CROSSREF.c2 (curmudgeon 2026-04-24). Two "Section 2.1" instances (in part2b and part6) are deliberately left as-is — no #p2-21 or section-2-1 anchor exists in part2.html, so the part-level fallback is the best available target until a 2.1 subsection anchor is added.
source_tracker_entry: baby-orphan-ISS-1521 (analyst-baby escalated 2026-05-14T13:39:04Z because mapping each of the 21 anchors to its correct subsection id requires Opus-level cross-referencing of the HTML id structure; PROP-029 safety-net orphan was claimed by baby and routed back to Opus as deep-attack).
review_class_rationale: These are 18 mechanical href substitutions against a verified anchor-id table. No new arguments, no new prose, no verdict changes — the work is closing-out content that has already been adversarially reviewed once (curmudgeon HOL-CROSSREF.c2 examined every link and identified these 21 by id). Curmudgeon at re-review only needs to confirm: (a) each replacement href points at a real id, (b) the link text still matches the target heading number, (c) no link text broke. Batchable with adjacent verification items per PROP-025 / curmudgeon-verify Step 8a. The decider class_hint was null (no hint), so I exercise PROP-025 author authority and declare verification.

patches.target_locator: part1.html: "Section 2.9" cross-reference, instance 1 (curmudgeon HOL-CROSSREF.c2 finding #3) — replace part-level fallback href="#part2" with subsection-anchor href="#2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop" so the delegated click handler scrolls the reader to the named subsection within the correct tab rather than dropping them at the top.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon HOL-CROSSREF.c2 finding #3 (severity=moderate) identified 21 part-level fallback hrefs where the link text claims a specific subsection (X.Y) but the href targets only the containing part. The delegated click handler in docs/index.html (a[href^="#"] addEventListener on window.load) reads the href, finds the element, walks up to its containing .ds-tab-content, switches to that tab with skipScroll, expands all ancestor <details> elements, and scrollIntoView()s the target. So changing href from "#part2" to "#2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop" routes the reader to the named subsection rather than the top of the tab. Pre-flight verified: locator unique within part1.html (apply-patches.js uses String.replace which replaces first occurrence; per-section uniqueness is sufficient). Anchor "2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop" verified to exist exactly once in part2.html (checked via id="..." regex scan).

BONUS: this instance also has a latent inline-onclick tab-routing bug — showTab('selftest') is invoked for a Section 7.2 link, but part7 belongs to the falsify tab, not selftest. The delegated handler currently silently corrects this by computing parentTab from the target element; the visible symptom is a brief flash of the wrong tab before the correct tab loads. Fixed inline as part of this same patch (showTab('selftest') → showTab('falsify')) since the patch already rewrites this exact <a> tag.

kernel_of_truth: The dome-side mirror of this critique is genuine: an internal documentation system that promises one destination and delivers another erodes reader trust, and the dome could (correctly) point at any such instance in our review as evidence that our methodology is not as careful as we claim it to be. Our cycle-1 OP curmudgeon pass declared 153/153 cross-references "clean," but cycle-2 found 7 semantic mismatches and 21 imprecise fallbacks — the document drifted because of piecemeal edits and the V6 restructure. The curmudgeon was right to surface this, even though zero of the 21 are functional bugs (the part-level href still navigates to the correct tab); it is the gap between what we promise readers and what we deliver that matters.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_dome: The kernel applies in reverse: this finding is OUR document's problem, not the dome's. The dome's parallel failure — hardcoded numbers in HTML, no derivation, accuracy claims that no script computes — is structural and unfixable by anchor cleanup. Our fix here is a 10-minute hygiene pass; the dome's equivalent problems require rebuilding the model. The fact that we are visibly fixing our own imprecise hrefs (and noting the fix in the methodology changelog) is exactly the "we audit our own document on a schedule and fix what we find" position the curmudgeon recommended in advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation.
holes_addressed: HOL-CROSSREF.c2 finding #3 (severity=moderate): 21 part-level fallback hrefs with subsection-claiming link text — 18 patched here (those with named subsection anchors), 2 deferred (Section 2.1 has no subsection anchor), 1 already fixed since the c2 audit (curmudgeon counted 21 on 2026-04-24, current state has 20)

BONUS: latent inline-onclick tab-routing bug on the part7/Section 7.2 link — showTab('selftest') invoked but section 7.2 belongs to falsify tab; fixed inline (showTab('selftest') → showTab('falsify')) since the patch already rewrites that exact <a> tag.
new_evidence_added: Anchor existence verified: #2-9-the-v13-coordinate-system-a-self-referential-loop (part2, 1 occurrence), #p6-61-0 (part6, 1), #p6-62-1 (part6, 1), #p6-66-5 (part6, 1), #p4-41-0 (part4, 1), #p7-72-1 (part7, 1) — all six target anchors confirmed present exactly once in their containing part via id="..." regex scan of data/sections.json.

Delegated handler verified: docs/index.html load-event listener on a[href^="#"] reads target id, walks closest(.ds-tab-content), calls showTab(tabId, {skipHash:true, skipScroll:true}), expandToElement (opens ancestor <details>), then setTimeout(scrollIntoView, 150). So an href="#p6-66-5" link with inline onclick="showTab('predictions');return false" correctly: (a) inline-onclick switches tab and scrolls to 0,0, (b) delegated handler re-switches to predictions, expands #p6-66-5's ancestor <details>, scrolls into view. Net: brief scroll-to-top flash, then scroll-to-subsection. No JS conflict.

Two "Section 2.1" instances deliberately left as-is: part2.html contains no id starting with "2-1-" or "p2-21" (verified via regex). The link text is honest about the destination at the tab level; until someone adds a 2.1 subsection anchor, the part-level fallback is the best available target.

anticipated_objections.objection: The inline onclick handlers will still call showTab() with the original tab argument and scroll to (0,0) before the delegated handler corrects. Why not also update the inline onclick to skipScroll, or drop it entirely?
anticipated_objections.response: Dropping the inline onclick would break no-JS-load-event scenarios (delegated handler is installed inside window.addEventListener("load", ...), so a click that happens BEFORE load completes would not be routed by the delegated handler — only the inline onclick is guaranteed to fire). Adding {skipScroll:true} to every inline onclick is a larger refactor that should be done as its own EXP. The brief scroll-to-top flash is a minor UX nit; the meaningful UX improvement (landing at the named subsection) lands with this patch alone. Conservative scope: change only the href.

anticipated_objections.objection: Two "Section 2.1" instances (part2b and part6) are still imprecise after this patch — why not also add the missing #section-2-1 anchor while you're at it?
anticipated_objections.response: Adding an anchor inside part2.html is a content edit (it requires deciding which heading in 2.1 to anchor and whether to add a new <h3 id="...">). That is out of scope for ISS-1521 (which is href cleanup, not anchor authoring). A separate ISS / EXP should propose the anchor addition; once it exists, a follow-up verification-class EXP can flip the two part-level fallbacks in the same one-line pattern.

anticipated_objections.objection: Replace-first-occurrence semantics: apply-patches.js uses String.replace(find, replace) which replaces only the FIRST match. Several patches in this set have identical bare locators (e.g., "Section 2.9" appears 10+ times in sections.json across multiple parts). Won't patches collide?
anticipated_objections.response: apply-patches.js targets a specific section by id (section_id="partN"), then runs replace on that section's html field only. The 10 "bare 2.9" instances are distributed across part5 (2), part6 (4), part7 (1), part8 (2), part1 (1). Within each part, the bare link text either has a unique-as-is occurrence or is given a 20-character context prefix to disambiguate. Patch ordering matters for partial uniqueness only: the patches in this set that target the SAME part but use different prefixes are constructed so that each prefix is unique within that part. Pre-flight: I verified each (current_text, target_section) pair has exactly one match in the current sections.json via node script — see "Pre-flight" block below.

preflight_verification.method: For each patch, ran content.indexOf(current_text) and content.indexOf(current_text, firstIdx+1) on the target section.html. Confirmed nextIdx === -1 for every patch (i.e., exactly one occurrence).

skipped_no_anchor.reason: No id starting with "2-1-" or "p2-21" exists in part2.html. Part-level fallback href="#part2" remains.
Same — no 2.1 subsection anchor exists in part2.html.

preflight_verification.delta_since_c2: Curmudgeon c2 audit (2026-04-24) counted 21 instances; current state (2026-05-17 clone) shows 20. One instance has been fixed by an intervening EXP/patch — likely the one that was on a high-traffic surface (would need a git log on data/sections.json since 2026-04-24 to identify which one, not blocking).

tldr_update_rationale: No section TLDRs change. These are href-only edits that swap the target anchor; link text, surrounding prose, and section TLDRs are all preserved verbatim.
newsworthy_rationale: Internal cross-reference hygiene — not a public-facing finding. Reader-visible effect is small (click "Section 6.6" → land on the §6.6 disclosure widget instead of the top of the predictions tab); meaningful for in-document navigation but not a discriminating argument against the dome.
follow_ups: OPTIONAL: drop inline onclick="showTab(...);return false" entirely on links that now have subsection-precise hrefs, OR add {skipScroll:true} to those onclicks, to avoid the brief scroll-to-top flash before the delegated handler scrolls to the subsection. Recommend as a separate verification-class EXP after this one lands.

NEEDED: add a #section-2-1 or #p2-21-N anchor inside part2.html so the two remaining "Section 2.1" → #part2 fallbacks can be tightened. Should be paired with an analyst pass on what the canonical 2.1 heading is currently called.

AUDIT REPRO: re-run the curmudgeon HOL-CROSSREF audit (c3) after this lands. The /tmp/*.js scripts captured in c2.methodology should re-run cleanly against the patched sections.json; expected counts: 0 critical, 0 major, ≤2 moderate (the two Section 2.1 cases).
summary_for_decider: EXP-408 closes ISS-1521 (HOL-CROSSREF.c2 finding #3 — 21 imprecise part-level hrefs) by surgically patching 18 of the 20 currently-present instances. 2 are deliberately deferred (no Section 2.1 subsection anchor exists in part2.html). 1 had already been fixed since the c2 audit. Each patch is a single-line href substitution with the same onclick and link text preserved; the one Section 7.2 patch additionally fixes a latent onclick tab-routing bug (showTab('selftest') → showTab('falsify') — section 7.2 lives on falsify tab, not selftest). review_class=verification (PROP-025) — mechanical href substitutions against a verified anchor-id table, no new prose. Pre-flight: every current_text locator is unique within its target section.html (verified via indexOf + nextIdx scan). Each new anchor id verified to exist exactly once in its containing part. Estimated curmudgeon re-review time: ~5 minutes (batchable with adjacent verification items).
id_reservation_note: Verified EXP-408 free in both clone (HEAD as of pull at run start) and FUSE (max EXP currently EXP-407). No collision expected.
